commands.review_summary:
The branch adds managed npm-root corruption detection/quarantine with one retry, changes post-core plugin convergence failures to warnings so update can restart Gateway, and updates CLI docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +113, Tests +162, Docs 0. Total +275 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Not as a live current-main repro in this read-only review; the linked report logs plus current-main source make the failure path source-reproducible, and the PR body now supplies after-fix packaged update proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair is apparent; the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the update semantics and final merge readiness review.

target.title: [codex] fix update plugin failure availability
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the update path that can leave Gateway down after a post-core plugin install failure.

## Root Cause

A managed npm plugin install can fail with local npm tree corruption signatures such as `ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE` or `ENOTEMPTY` while mutating `node_modules`. The updater then converted plugin-scoped post-core convergence failures into a top-level update error, so the package update could finish but the managed Gateway restart path was skipped.

## Changes

- Detect npm managed-root corruption signatures, including `ENOTEMPTY` tree mutation failures.
- Quarantine only rebuild artifacts (`node_modules`, `package-lock.json`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`) and retry the managed npm install once.
- Keep post-core plugin convergence failures as plugin warnings so the updated Gateway still restarts.
- Update CLI docs for the new behavior.

Related: #85113

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: a package-manager `openclaw update` with a corrupt managed plugin should keep the core update successful, report the plugin problem as `postUpdate.plugins.status: "warning"`, and still restart the updated managed Gateway.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout at PR head. The proof used a temp HOME, temp npm global prefix, temp OpenClaw state dir, and unique temporary LaunchAgent profile/label. It installed published `openclaw@latest` as the baseline package, built a PR package tarball from this branch, installed a fixture npm plugin from a local registry, corrupted that plugin payload, then ran the real packaged update path. The default user OpenClaw profile/state was not used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the package with `node scripts/package-openclaw-for-docker.mjs`, installed baseline `openclaw@latest`, ran `openclaw gateway install --force --json` for the temporary profile, installed `npm:@openclaw/demo-corrupt-plugin@0.0.1`, removed that plugin's `package.json`, then ran `openclaw update --channel beta --tag <PR package tarball> --yes --json` without `--no-restart`. After update returned, probed the temporary Gateway `/healthz` endpoint and captured `openclaw gateway status --json`.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "update_exit": "0",
  "update_status": "ok",
  "update_mode": "npm",
  "after_version": "2026.5.28",
  "post_update_plugin_status": "warning",
  "warning_plugin_id": "demo-corrupt-plugin",
  "guidance": [
    "Run openclaw doctor --fix to attempt automatic repair.",
    "Run openclaw plugins inspect demo-corrupt-plugin --runtime --json for details."
  ],
  "outcome_status": "error",
  "gateway_healthz_reachable_after_restart": true,
  "service_loaded_after_restart": true
}
```

The managed npm root recovery helper was also exercised against a real temp filesystem-backed npm project containing stale `@openclaw/discord/node_modules/undici/types` payload:

```text
detected=true
moved=node_modules,package-lock.json
node_modules_exists_after=false
package_lock_exists_after=false
package_json_preserved=true
archive_preserved=true
stale_payload_quarantined=true
```

- Observed result after fix: the real packaged update exited 0, preserved top-level `status: "ok"`, reported the corrupt plugin through structured warning guidance, restarted the temporary managed Gateway, and the restarted Gateway answered `/healthz`. The temporary LaunchAgent was removed during cleanup; a post-cleanup `launchctl print` for the temporary label returned not found.
- What was not tested: the Docker corrupt-plugin lane could not run in this local environment because Docker is not installed. The equivalent local packaged update/restart path above was run against a temp macOS LaunchAgent instead.

## Validation

- Local packaged update/restart proof described above.
- `pnpm test src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- Attempted `pnpm test:docker:update-corrupt-plugin`; package build and tarball integrity passed, then the lane stopped before the scenario because Docker is unavailable locally.
